order-by.label: ORDER BY Clause
order-by.summary: Sort query results by one or more columns.

sketch-what-is-order-by.title: What is ORDER BY?
sketch-what-is-order-by.bodyMarkdown: The `ORDER BY` clause in SQL is used to sort the results of a query by one or more columns. By default, sorting is in ascending order, but you can specify descending order as well. This helps you organize your data output in a meaningful way, such as alphabetically or by numerical value.

sketch-order-by-syntax.title: ORDER BY Syntax
sketch-order-by-syntax.bodyMarkdown:
The basic syntax for using `ORDER BY` is:

```sql
SELECT column1, column2 FROM table_name
ORDER BY column1 [ASC|DESC], column2 [ASC|DESC];
```
You can list multiple columns to sort by, and specify `ASC` for ascending or `DESC` for descending order.

quiz-order-by-basic-single-choice.title: Purpose of ORDER BY
quiz-order-by-basic-single-choice.prompt: What is the main purpose of the ORDER BY clause in SQL?
quiz-order-by-basic-single-choice.hint: Think about how you would arrange results alphabetically or numerically.

help.concept: The ORDER BY clause is used to arrange the output of a query in a specific order based on one or more columns.
help.hint_1: ORDER BY does not filter or group data; it arranges the output.
help.hint_2: It is used to sort the results, not to limit or group them.

options.a: To filter rows based on a condition
options.b: To group rows with the same values
options.c: To sort the result set by one or more columns
options.d: To limit the number of rows returned

quiz-order-by-syntax-fill-blank.title: ORDER BY Syntax
quiz-order-by-syntax-fill-blank.prompt:
Fill in the blank to complete the SQL statement for sorting employees by salary in descending order:

```sql
SELECT name, salary FROM employees_basic
____ salary DESC;
```
quiz-order-by-syntax-fill-blank.hint: Which clause is used for sorting results?

help.concept: the missing term is the SQL clause used to sort query results by specified columns.
help.hint_1: GROUP BY is for grouping, WHERE is for filtering, LIMIT is for restricting row count.
help.hint_2: The clause for sorting is placed after the SELECT and FROM statements.

quiz-order-by-syntax-fill-blank.template:
SELECT name, salary FROM employees_basic
{{blank}} salary DESC;

quiz-order-by-drag-reorder.title: Arrange the SQL Query
quiz-order-by-drag-reorder.prompt: Put the parts of this SQL query in the correct order to select all products and sort them by price in ascending order.
quiz-order-by-drag-reorder.hint: Start with what you want to select, then the table, then how to sort.

help.concept: A typical SQL query starts with SELECT, followed by FROM, and then ORDER BY if sorting is needed.
help.hint_1: SELECT comes first, then FROM, then ORDER BY.
help.hint_2: The sorting instruction (ORDER BY) is always at the end of the query.

quiz-order-by-code-input.title: Write an ORDER BY Query
quiz-order-by-code-input.prompt: Write a SQL query to select the name and grade from the students_intro table, sorted by grade in descending order.
quiz-order-by-code-input.hint: Use ORDER BY with the grade column and specify descending order.

help.concept: ORDER BY lets you sort results by a column, and DESC sorts in descending order.
help.hint_1: Add ORDER BY after the FROM clause, using the column you want to sort by.
help.hint_2: Remember to specify DESC for descending order after the column name.

quiz-order-by-code-input.starterCode:
SELECT name, grade FROM students_intro
-- Add sorting here
